_placement_note: kot-assess/1 schema per docs/next/research-engine.md SS2.1. That section's candidates/<id>/assessment/ namespace (programme-2 deltas D1/D3) is not yet built, so this record lives at registry/assessments/<exp-id>.json alongside registry/verdicts/ and registry/audits/, tracked by registry/audit-status.jsonl per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 3. First kot-assess record in the repo; candidate name 'verifier-offload' is the SS2.1 name for the F2 line (see the f2b-replicate record title).
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4: the run (Opus runner-3/4/5) produced only the mechanical verdict and the cross-vendor audit; what the outcome MEANS is decided here and only here.
_surprise_note: No prereg prior was registered (pre-D1: no claim.md exists for this candidate), and the frozen record's STIPULATED oracle-leakage assumption explicitly declined to presume REAL vs NOT-LEAKY ('built to separate REAL from NOT-LEAKY, not to presume either'). Informally, F2's 272-up/0-down flip profile had made the leakage/structure-artifact reading the live suspicion, so a shuffled-recovery point estimate of -0.021 is a high-information outcome; but with no registered prior, no 'surprise-*' tag is licensed. The information content is captured in questions_opened priority instead.

interpretation.kill_chain_read: None of the three verbatim kills fired. (a) primary: PASSED, effect_size = +0.1507 with one-sided 95% BCa lower bound +0.1053 >= 0 [MEASURED]. (b) shuffled recovery: point estimate -0.0206, far below the 0.30 point-estimate kill trigger; the Holm secondary also passed (ub95 = 0.1076 < 0.30) [MEASURED]. (c) commodity-verification: competitor_closes_asmuch = false — gloss self-verify+retry 0.4893 and Skywork PRM-1.5B 0.5267 vs kernel-verify 0.7507 at <= matched FLOPs/query (F0 ledger) [MEASURED]. The TOST/NULL branch was never reached (tost_equivalence_pass = false). Separation instrument valid (R3-R1 gap 0.108, one-sided LB 0.072 > 0); extraction instrument 500 labelled / 0 failures; both ratio secondaries therefore read and both passed. Seed sign 3/3 (reported-only). The verdict rule that fired is the full PASS conjunction: primary AND beats_gloss AND beats_text_null AND shuffled_low_recovery.
licenses: [MEASURED] HE1 (fresh-item replication, absolute non-inferiority): on 250 fresh, LC8-leak-checked, kernel-covered d-qa-r items x 3 seeds, SmolLM2-135M + kernel-verify-retry (fixed pre-registered k=4) reached acc 0.7507 vs SmolLM2-1.7B-alone 0.6000 — absolute effect +0.1507 (one-sided 95% BCa LB +0.1053). The F2-family verifier lift is real on fresh items in the denominator-free form; the F2 primary's death is now attributable to its degenerate gap denominator, not to the absence of a lift, WITHIN THIS SCOPE.

[MEASURED] H-STRUCT (the load-bearing new result): a seed-pinned DERANGEMENT of the record->concept map at identical retry topology and identical cost (shuffled arm acc 0.4867 vs R1-alone 0.4920) recovered -2.1% (point) of the true-verify lift, ub95 +10.8% < 30%. The lift requires the CORRECT record<->item alignment: it is kernel-CONTENT-specific, not retry-against-any-oracle STRUCTURE. The structure-artifact explanation of F2's 272-up/0-down flip profile is dead at this scope.

[MEASURED] RT-2 + Law-2 at this scope: the kernel arm beats gloss-text self-verify+retry at matched budget (0.4893) and the passive kernel-as-text null (0.4920, indistinguishable from R1-alone). Passive kernel prose confers no lift here; the benefit requires the machine-checkable verify-retry mechanism.

[MEASURED] HC3, indexed to this PRM size class only: the kernel verifier beats the trained Skywork-o1-Open-PRM-Qwen-2.5-1.5B (pinned revision) at matched FLOPs (PRM arm 0.5267). The commodity-verification kill did not fire at the 1.5B class.

[MEASURED] Ratio secondaries (separation gate valid): gap_closed(R1,R3) = 2.40 with one-sided LB 1.76 > 1.0 — the 135M+verifier system does not merely close the R1->R3 gap on this slice, it overshoots it.

[MEASURED] Efficiency datum (F0 ledger, this eval design): cost_ratio_vs_R3 = 0.103 — the winning 135M+verifier arm ran at ~10% of the 1.7B-alone per-query cost while beating it by 15.1 points absolute. Supports the efficiency thesis at exactly this scope.

[MEASURED] Verdict standing: audit-confirmed PASS (cross-vendor Codex/GPT-5.5 GATE-A recompute, all 12 checks ok). Per the engine's PASS defaults (research-engine.md SS2.2) the verifier-offload candidate is PROMOTED for exactly this claim scope, and the gated f2b-TRANSFER experiment is UNLOCKED — this replicate was its gate.
does_not_license: [ENVELOPE-BOUND] SIGN, not slope: '2 host rungs (135M, 1.7B) license a SIGN, not a slope' (extrapolation_envelope_verbatim). No scaling claim of any form; scale_language_licensed = sign-only.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Scope, verbatim: 'every claim is scoped to <=1.7B hosts, the kernel-covered SELF-AUTHORED item slice (templated definitional QA over the 108 covered concepts), this PRM size class (1.5B, pinned revision), and an oracle-favourable eval design'. The +0.1507 effect must not be quoted outside this scope; a MEASURED number cited outside its envelope re-classifies as EXTRAPOLATION (engine law 9).

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Shrinking catch-room: 'the s->S gap itself narrows as S grows, so absolute verifier catch-room shrinks with scale'. The absolute lift at larger hosts is expected to be smaller and is strictly unmeasured.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Coverage: 'kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0 — MEASURED by m0b on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance, NOT general coverage'. Every accuracy claim is bounded to the covered slice; ~35% expressibility on one rung of one instance bounds the deployable surface and licenses no whole-distribution claim.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Above 7B: 'cascade/verification-routing literature licenses mechanism existence above 7B, never effect size' (LIT-BACKED existence only); and 'the HC3 verdict is indexed to the PRM size class tested and a frontier-scale PRM re-opens the fork'.

[DESIGN-BOUND] NOT a transfer result: the d-ext external slice was DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — all 500 d-ext items have kernel_checkable = false by construction, so that arm measured off-coverage abstention behaviour, not transfer. Nothing in this PASS says the lift survives items not authored against the kernel; that is f2b-TRANSFER.

[DESIGN-BOUND] Content-specificity is not ground-truth independence — the sharpest residual confound: H-STRUCT separates CONTENT from STRUCTURE, not real semantics from definitional circularity. The verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical record, and d-qa-r gold is still DEFINED by that same equality (templates and the 108 covered concepts are shared with d-qa by construction; freshness lives in distractors, donor claims, segments, option orders). Under BOTH the real-content reading AND the self-consistent-circularity reading, a derangement destroys the lift — so the shuffled control cannot adjudicate between them. Only externally-authored kernel-covered items (f2b-TRANSFER / RT-7a) can. This is why the envelope says 'oracle-favourable eval design' and why f2b-TRANSFER is the foreground next question, not an optional extension.

[BINDING-NOTE] F2 is untouched: this record supersedes-in-spirit only. The frozen F2 record, its FAIL verdict, and its confirmatory secondaries (including the HE2 cascade-dominance failure) stand exactly as issued; cascades are not re-litigated by this PASS.

epistemic_promotions.claim: the F2 verifier lift replicates on fresh items (absolute non-inferiority vs 1.7B-alone)
the lift is kernel-content-specific (not retry-against-any-oracle structure)
epistemic_promotions.scope: same envelope; does NOT extend to ground-truth independence (see does_not_license)

epistemic_promotions.claim: any statement about hosts > 1.7B, non-templated items, externally-authored items, frontier PRMs, or general coverage

forks_resolved.resolution: SUPPORTED (absolute non-inferiority form): effect +0.1507, LB95 +0.1053 >= 0 on fresh kernel-covered items with a valid separation instrument. Sign-only, envelope-scoped.

SUPPORTED for kernel CONTENT over retry STRUCTURE: shuffled derangement at identical topology/cost recovers -0.021 (point; ub95 0.108 < 0.30). The residual authoring-circularity branch is explicitly NOT resolved (deferred to f2b-TRANSFER by design).
RESOLVED AT THIS PRM CLASS ONLY: kernel verifier beats the 1.5B trained PRM at matched FLOPs (0.7507 vs 0.5267). Fork re-opens at a frontier-scale PRM per the envelope.
SUPPORTED: beats gloss-text self-verify+retry at matched budget (0.7507 vs 0.4893) and the passive text null (0.4920).

questions_opened.question: FOREGROUND — does the verifier lift transfer to kernel-covered items NOT authored against the kernel (RT-7a: externally-authored, kernel-checkable items)? This is the only experiment that can separate real kernel content from definitional circularity (gold == canonical-record equality), the sharpest residual confound of this PASS. This replicate was f2b-TRANSFER's gate; the gate is now open.
blocking: verifier-offload promotion beyond the self-authored slice

questions_opened.question: Third host rung (engine PASS default: rung-extension): convert the sign into a slope and test the envelope's catch-room-shrinkage prediction (s->S gap narrows as S grows). Effect size above 1.7B is strictly unmeasured.

Falsification-hardening (engine PASS default: attack-your-winner): re-match HC3 against a larger/frontier PRM class at matched FLOPs. The commodity-verification kill remains live outside the 1.5B class per the envelope.

Absorption probe (engine PASS default for injected mechanisms, N0 Law 3 / InstructRetro expectation): does fine-tuning the 135M host on kernel content absorb the verifier's benefit, i.e. is the lift a persistent architectural seam or a stopgap for missing weights?

Coverage instrument: 0.3542 at molecules-v0 on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance bounds the deployable surface. Grow the kernel and/or measure coverage at further rungs; every f2-line accuracy claim inherits this bound.

Template-style generalisation: d-qa-r shares the 108 covered concepts and the fixed question templates with d-qa by construction (freshness lives in distractors/donor claims/segments/option orders). Does the lift survive non-templated phrasings of the same covered content?

_questions_note: est_cost_usd / est_agent_days are STIPULATED planning estimates (dry-plan style), never measurements. Priority order is as listed; f2b-transfer-go is deliberately first and cheap — it attacks the confound this design could not close.

ledger_updates.scope: <=1.7B hosts; self-authored kernel-covered templated definitional QA (108 covered concepts); k=4; oracle-favourable eval design; sign-only (2 rungs)

ledger_updates.statement: Seed-pinned derangement of the record->concept map at identical retry topology and cost recovers ~0 of the lift (point -0.0206; ub95 0.1076 < 0.30): the lift is kernel-content-specific, not retry-against-any-oracle structure.
At matched budget/FLOPs, none of gloss-text self-verify+retry (0.4893), trained Skywork PRM-1.5B (0.5267), or passive kernel-as-text (0.4920) closes the lift (kernel-verify 0.7507).
ledger_updates.scope: same as above; HC3 component indexed to the 1.5B PRM class, pinned revision

ledger_updates.statement: Separation instrument valid: acc(R3-alone) - acc(R1-alone) = 0.108, one-sided 95% BCa LB 0.072 > 0. Extraction instrument: 500 labelled, 0 failures. Seed sign consistent 3/3 (reported-only).

ledger_updates.statement: F2's 272-up/0-down flip profile is NOT explained by retry-against-any-oracle structure (shuffled control). The definitional-circularity explanation (gold DEFINED by the same string-equality the verifier checks, on self-authored items) remains OPEN and is exactly what f2b-TRANSFER tests.
